rulings.Case Title: 629 POST ST., LLC VS. SANSANEE SAEJEAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 22, 2015, Line 7: PLAINTIFF 629 POST ST., LLC'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment For Possession. GRANTED. Defendant addressed the motion on the merits. Parties entered into a binding agreement as reflected in the Transcript. Defendant acknowledged that agreement was understood and accepted as stated in the Stipulation, supplemented by additional terms on the record. Defendant stipulated to sign Uniform Hotel Visitor Policy as attached to the Settlement. Defendant agreed to be bound by the policy, except Defendant received additional 4 nights for overnight guest stay. Per the terms of the Stipulation Plaintiff was not obligated to prepare a modified Visitor Policy; Plaintiff did not agree to keep a running real-time list of Defendant's overnight visitors. Stipulation did not provide for an opportunity to cure; stipulation provided for a notice of CCP 664.6 enforcement motion/application in paragraph 8A. Defendant had guests for 15 nights and visitors who did not provide proper ID contrary to the stipulation to abide by Visitor Policy. =(501/AJR/ late tentative)

rulings.Case Title: 946 JONES STREET, LLC VS. SOMDEE VIBOONVORADEJ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Prayer For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 22, 2015, Line 10: DEFENDANT SOMDEE VIBOONVORADEJ'S Motion To Strike Prayer For Attorney's Fees. DENIED. Parties' relationship is governed by the provisions of the Lease (including paragraphs 6 and 10) since its inception and until the termination of the tenancy by Ellis Act or other proper means. The contract provides for surrender of the premises upon termination of the lease (here by Ellis Act) and for recovery of attorney's fees. "It is therefore necessary to analyze and determine whether or not the right to possession by the landlord ... had its inception in a lease or contractual right or whether the right to possession by the landlord had its inception in some sort of tortious conduct by the lessee." Mitchell Land and Imp. Co v. Ristorante Ferrantelli, Inc. (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 479. Moving party fails to point to any allegations of "tortious conduct" in the Complaint, thus Drybread is distinguishable. (Drybread v. Chipain Chiropractic Corp. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 1063. No cases cited for the proposition that under the circumstances of this case, i.e. statutory termination of unexpired tenancy, attorney's fees are not recoverable. =(501/AJR/late tentative)
